subject: Fix nodeSlector and Affinity for aai charts
commit_message:
Fix nodeSlector and Affinity for aai charts

Indentation problem. Correctly placed nodeSelector and Affinity
aligned with Contrainers in template.spec
common.resources do not need indent as indent 12 already present
in return value

Change-Id: Ie0e84fba2f18d903fdcc8bb8f6ff06a4785c0a75
Issue-ID: OOM-1541
Signed-off-by: Kranthi Guttikonda <kranthi.guttikonda@b-yond.com>
Signed-off-by: kranthikirang <kranthi.guttikonda@b-yond.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/1374/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/1374

Patch Set 1:

(4 comments)

This resources section is for side car container. We provide nodeSelector for all containers and resources for each container.

Patch Set 1:

Patch Set 1:

I think I found the problem. It is before this patch, probably unrelated:
# helm upgrade -i aaa-aai local/aai --namespace onap -f ~/global-values.yaml --set global.pullPolicy=IfNotPresent,aai.enabled=true

Actually this fix has nothing to do with aai functionality. This only applicable when NodeSelector is used. Also resources are properly placed for all containers based on flavor defined. I am not sure why its failing actually

Patch Set 1:

I think I found the problem.
It is in sparky-be deployment
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        - containerPort:

In manifest it is:
        ports:
        - containerPort: {{ .Values.service.internalPort }}
        - containerPort: {{ .Values.service.internalPort2 }}
while internalPort2 is not defined.
